architecture.key_insight: content/* = feature modules (what the user sees), foundation/* = shared engine (how it works)

packages.content: Feature modules: kinetics, logistics, trains, schematics, fluids, redstone, contraptions, processing, equipment, decoration, materials
packages.foundation: Shared infrastructure: block, blockEntity, item, gui, networking, mixin, render, model, particle, utility, advancement, data, ponder

registration.pattern: Every registration type has a single All*.java file at the root package level. Each file contains a static init block or method. Items reference blocks by their AllBlocks constants.
registration.example: AllItems extends AllBlocks — items are created from blocks in one line: public static final Item COGWHEEL = new BlockItem(AllBlocks.COGWHEEL, ...)

key_patterns.name: Content Module Pattern
key_patterns.description: Each feature lives in content/<feature>/ with its own block/, blockEntity/, renderer/ sub-packages. This is the most scalable pattern for large mods.
key_patterns.example: content/kinetics/ contains all rotation-related blocks, entities, and rendering

key_patterns.name: Foundation as Framework
key_patterns.description: foundation/ contains abstract base classes and utilities that ALL content modules use. This is like an internal framework.
key_patterns.example: foundation/blockEntity/SyncedBlockEntity.java — base class all block entities extend

key_patterns.name: Root-Level All*.java Registration
key_patterns.description: Unlike Farmer's Delight's registry/ subpackage, Create puts ALL registrations at the ROOT level. This works better for very large mods where every class needs to reference registrations without deep imports.
